turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been having chest tightness for the past 3 days, feels like a pressure sensation. It's been getting worse and I'm having trouble breathing when I exert myself.

turns.student: Can you tell me more about what you've been feeling?
turns.patient: It starts in my chest, just below my breastbone. When it happens, I feel this heavy, squeezing pressure that makes it hard to breathe. It's not a sharp pain, but more like a dull ache. It comes and goes, usually lasts about 10-15 minutes each time, but it's been happening more often lately.

turns.student: When you have those episodes, do you notice anything else happening to your body?
turns.patient: Yeah, I've noticed that sometimes when the tightness is really bad, my left arm feels numb and tingly. It's just for a second or two, but it's definitely there. And like I mentioned earlier, I get pretty sweaty during those episodes too.

turns.student: Any sweating or clamminess when the symptoms hit?
turns.patient: Yeah, I do get pretty sweaty during those episodes. Like I said before, it comes with this heavy pressure sensation in my chest and makes breathing really hard.

turns.student: So no perspiration at all during the episodes?
turns.patient: Yeah, like I already mentioned, I do get sweaty when it happens. It's not just a little bit either, I'm pretty drenched by the time the episode is over.

turns.student: Does the discomfort stay in one spot, or does it move around?
turns.patient: The tightness stays right in my chest, just below my breastbone. Sometimes when it's really bad, it feels like it spreads a bit to my left arm and back, but it's still mostly concentrated in my chest.

turns.student: Does it radiate anywhere — like your arms, neck, or jaw?
turns.patient: The tightness stays right in my chest, just below my breastbone. Sometimes when it's really bad, it feels like it spreads a bit to my left arm and back, but it's still mostly concentrated in my chest. Like I said earlier, during those episodes, I also get this numbness or tingling sensation in my left arm, but only for a second or two.

turns.student: Any tingling or numbness in your arms when it happens?
turns.patient: Yeah, like I already mentioned, sometimes when the tightness is really bad, my left arm feels numb and tingly. It's just for a second or two, but it's definitely there.

turns.student: What about your extremities — any tingling, pain, or unusual sensations?
turns.patient: No, other than the occasional numbness in my left arm during those episodes, I don't get any weird sensations in my arms or legs.

turns.student: Have you noticed any swelling anywhere? Ankles, feet, hands?
turns.patient: Yeah, actually, my ankles and shoes feel a bit tighter than usual lately. But it's not like they're swollen or anything, just feels like I've gained some extra water weight.

turns.student: Do your shoes feel tighter than usual, or any puffiness in your legs?
turns.patient: Yeah, actually, my ankles and shoes feel a bit tighter than usual lately. But it's not like they're swollen or anything, just feels like I've gained some extra water weight.

turns.student: Any stomach issues during the episodes? Nausea, queasiness, upset stomach?
turns.patient: Yeah, mild nausea during those episodes, no vomiting. It's pretty uncomfortable, but it goes away once the tightness subsides.

turns.student: Do you feel sick to your stomach when the symptoms come on?
turns.patient: Yeah, I do get a bit of nausea when the chest tightness is really bad, like I mentioned earlier. It's not severe enough to make me want to vomit or anything, but it's definitely there and adds to my overall discomfort.

turns.student: Have you had any fevers, chills, or night sweats?
turns.patient: No, I haven't experienced any fever, chills, or night sweats. My symptoms have been pretty consistent with just chest tightness, shortness of breath, and some nausea during those episodes.

turns.student: Have you noticed any changes in your appetite or weight?
turns.patient: Honestly, I haven't really thought about my appetite much lately, but I don't think it's changed. As for weight, I've been trying to keep an eye on it since I started feeling this way, and I'm pretty sure I've gained a bit, but nothing too drastic. My wife has mentioned that I seem to be eating more snacks when we're out on the road driving together.

turns.student: Tell me about your lifestyle — diet, exercise, daily routine.
turns.patient: To be honest, my lifestyle isn't exactly the healthiest. As a long-haul truck driver, I'm on the road most of the week and I don't have much time for regular meals or exercise. My diet is pretty heavy in fast food - I'd say 5-6 days out of the week I eat at least one meal from a restaurant that serves fried foods and burgers. And when I do get a break, I tend to grab whatever's quick and easy, like processed snacks or energy drinks. As for exercise, I'm pretty sedentary - I don't really have time for it with my schedule.

turns.student: How active are you physically? Do you work out or walk regularly?
turns.patient: Honestly, I'd say I'm quite inactive. My job as a truck driver doesn't exactly lend itself to regular exercise. When I do get breaks, I usually try to stretch my legs and move around for a bit, but that's about it. Other than that, I don't really have time or motivation to work out regularly.

turns.student: What does a typical day of eating look like for you?
turns.patient: Honestly, my diet is pretty heavy in fast food - I'd say 5-6 days out of the week I eat at least one meal from a restaurant that serves fried foods and burgers. And when I do get a break, I tend to grab whatever's quick and easy, like processed snacks or energy drinks. Like I said earlier, as a long-haul truck driver, my schedule is pretty irregular, and it makes it hard for me to plan healthy meals in advance.

turns.student: Any medical problems in your family? Parents, siblings?
turns.patient: My mother has hypertension and type 2 diabetes. She's still alive at 78 years old, which I'm grateful for.

turns.student: Specifically, has anyone in your family had serious diseases?
turns.patient: My father had a heart attack when he was 52 years old and passed away. I've always tried to be more mindful of my own health because of that, but like I said earlier, my lifestyle hasn't been exactly conducive to staying healthy.
